author: Ernie
title: Montana & Hawaii: Two Paths Against Citizens United
summary: A verified snapshot comparing Montana's ballot-initiative path (Initiative 194) with Hawaii's legislative test-case path (Act 11).

tracks_overview.title: Citizens United Legal/Political History
points: How it enabled Super PAC/independent-expenditure power.
What states can and cannot regulate after Citizens United.

tracks_overview.title: Current Anti–Citizens United Reform Efforts
points: Maine: voters approved a 2024 measure limiting contributions to Super PACs; resulting litigation is a major test case.
American Promise is pushing a federal constitutional amendment to restore authority over campaign-finance rules.

tracks_overview.title: Ballot Initiatives Currently Circulating
points: Missouri has 2026 initiative petitions approved for circulation, including direct-democracy protection language.
Florida maintains an official active constitutional-initiative database.
California lists initiatives cleared for circulation.

research_context.comparison_rationale: Montana = ballot initiative path. Hawaii = legislative test-case path. These two together are the right comparison: one people-powered ballot route, one legislature-enacted route.

measure.title: Exclude Political Spending from Artificial Persons' Charter Powers Initiative
measure.description: Would prohibit artificial persons from contributing to campaigns, ballot-measure elections, or political parties.
measure.status: Signatures submitted; county verification in progress

sources.label: Montana Secretary of State — Petition Signatures

hawaii.name: Hawaii
hawaii.note: Hawaii is not a citizen ballot initiative state in the same way Montana is.

measure.title: Corporate Charter Restrictions on Election Spending
measure.description: Uses corporate-charter theory to restrict corporations and other artificial entities from election spending. Expected to face First Amendment litigation.

framing.supporters: Frame Hawaii as a test state for undoing Citizens United through state corporate law.
framing.critics: Argue the law likely conflicts with Supreme Court precedent.

sources.label: AP News — Hawaii Corporate Influence Law
Center for American Progress — Hawaii Analysis

related_references.label: Maine Question 1 — Limit Contributions to Super PACs (2024)
American Promise — For Our Freedom Amendment

bottom_line: Montana = ballot initiative path. Hawaii = legislative test-case path. These two together are exactly the right comparison: one people-powered ballot route, one legislature-enacted route.
